step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with agent1’s assessment. Reinforcing glass ionomer cement with metal particles does indeed enhance its mechanical integrity by improving its resistance to abrasion. Additionally, this modification can also lead to better long-term stability since increased wear resistance means less material loss over time, preserving the structural integrity longer than standard glass ionomers might provide alone. 

Therefore, my final answer remains consistent as well: **B - Increased resistance to abrasion**.
